epics.title: Code Editing
epics.description: Core syntax-highlighted editor with file open/save, undo/redo, multi-tab support.

epics.title: File Management
epics.description: File Explorer with full directory CRUD — browse, create, rename, delete, move.

epics.title: Terminal
epics.description: Integrated sandboxed terminal via Xterm.js + WASI for running build/test commands.

epics.title: Command Palette
epics.description: Fuzzy-search command palette accessible by gesture or Cmd+P for keyboard-free power workflows.

epics.title: Customization
epics.description: Light/dark themes, font size settings, and extension installation.

epics.title: Plan Visualizer
epics.description: Self-updating HTML dashboard for project plan status, cost tracking, and traceability — auto-deployed to GitHub Pages.

epics.title: Authentication
epics.description: GitHub OAuth sign-in via browser redirect, secure token storage in platform keychain, and sign-out with token revocation.

epics.title: Git Integration
epics.description: Full git workflow via isomorphic-git — clone, stage, commit, push, pull, diff, and branch management.

epics.title: In-App Purchases & Monetization
epics.description: Three-tier subscription model (Free / Pro / Pro+AI) via native IAP with receipt validation and feature gating.

epics.title: AI Suggestions
epics.description: Inline code completions and AI chat panel powered by Claude API, gated behind the Pro+AI subscription tier.

epics.title: App Store & EAS Build Delivery
epics.description: EAS Build pipeline producing iOS .ipa and Android .aab artefacts, submitted to App Store Connect and Google Play Console.

epics.title: Cloud Sync
epics.description: S3-compatible cloud storage for project sync — upload on save, pull on launch, conflict resolution UI.

epics.title: Multi-Language Editor Support
epics.description: Per-language indentation rules, auto-close behaviour, and comprehensive VSCode-compatible extension coverage (C, C++, C#, F#, TypeScript, JavaScript, Python, Rust, Go, Swift, Dart/Flutter, Lua, Elixir, R, Scala, Perl, Terraform, PowerShell, and more).

epics.title: Global Search — Find in Files
epics.description: Cross-project full-text search panel with result grouping by file, line preview, and navigate-to-line.

epics.title: Crash Reporting & Observability
epics.description: Automated crash reporting via Sentry/Bugsnag and performance metric tracking (cold start, latency, memory) with alerting.

epics.title: Native iOS Folder Picker
epics.description: Custom native module wrapping UIDocumentPickerViewController in directory mode with security-scoped URL bookmarks, allowing users to pick workspace folders outside the app sandbox (iCloud Drive, On My iPad, external providers) with persistent write access.

epics.title: Advanced Git Workflows
epics.description: Branch create/switch UI, merge conflict resolution editor, git gutter indicators, stash management, and git blame — completing a professional git workflow on mobile.

epics.title: Advanced Editor Features
epics.description: Search & replace across files, hardware keyboard shortcuts (⌘S / ⌘` / ⌘N / ⌘P), code folding, Prettier auto-format, breadcrumbs navigation, and snippet expansion.

epics.title: Code Navigation
epics.description: Go to Definition, Find All References, Peek Definition, and workspace symbol search — powered by Monaco's built-in AST support before full LSP lands.

epics.title: AI Code Intelligence
epics.description: Inline AI edit (Cmd+K equivalent), project-scoped AI rules, AI-generated commit messages, and multi-model provider support (Claude, GPT-4, Gemini) — extends EPIC-0010 with intelligence beyond raw completions.

epics.title: Language Server Protocol
epics.description: LSP worker integration delivering real IntelliSense completions, hover documentation, and real-time error diagnostics with inline squiggles for TypeScript, Python, and Go.

epics.title: AI Codebase Indexing
epics.description: Semantic vector indexing of the open workspace enabling @file and @codebase context references in AI chat/completions, with a privacy mode to disable indexing for sensitive repos.

epics.title: Mobile Debugging
epics.description: Breakpoints, watch expressions, call stack panel, and debug console — adapted for mobile touch interaction and targeting JS/TS runtimes via WASI debug adapters.

epics.title: Collaboration & Sharing
epics.description: Settings sync across devices, shareable code snippet links, and a lightweight Live Share-style session for real-time collaborative editing (viewer + single editor).

stories.title: As a developer, I want to open a file on my device, so that I can read and edit existing code.

acs.text: FileExplorer displays the device file tree in the sidebar
Tapping a file loads its content into the editor
Active file path is displayed in the editor path bar

stories.title: As a developer, I want to edit code with syntax highlighting, so that I can read code more easily.

acs.text: TypeScript/JavaScript files receive correct syntax highlighting (language detected from extension)
Editor is responsive to touch input — tap positions cursor, pinch adjusts font size
Virtual keyboard raises the editor viewport so the cursor line remains visible

stories.title: As a developer, I want to save my changes, so that my work is not lost.

acs.text: Cmd+S / toolbar Save triggers onSave with the active file path and current content
After save, the dirty indicator (●) is removed from the active tab
Saving a file with no changes (clean tab) calls onSave without error

stories.title: As a developer, I want to undo and redo changes, so that I can recover from mistakes.

acs.text: Pressing the Undo toolbar button sends the undo command to Monaco without throwing
Pressing the Redo toolbar button sends the redo command to Monaco without throwing

stories.title: As a developer, I want to search and replace text within a file, so that I can refactor code quickly.

acs.text: Pressing the Find toolbar button sends the find command to Monaco without throwing
Find toolbar button is accessible via aria-label and has a minimum 44pt touch target

stories.title: As a developer, I want to view multiple files in tabs, so that I can work across files without losing context.

acs.text: Tab bar renders one tab per open file with correct label
Tapping a tab calls onTabChange with that file's path
Pressing the × close button calls onTabClose with the correct path
Dirty tabs show ● prefix; clean tabs show no indicator

stories.title: As a developer, I want to browse my project directory tree, so that I can find and open any file.

acs.text: FileExplorer renders all file and directory entries returned by FileSystemBridge
Tapping a directory expands it and shows its children; tapping again collapses it
Long-pressing any entry opens a context menu with New File, New Folder, Rename, Move to, and Delete options

stories.title: As a developer, I want to create new files and folders, so that I can add to my project.

acs.text: New file created at correct path + tree refreshed (TC-0041..TC-0048)
New folder created at correct path + tree refreshed (TC-0049..TC-0052)

stories.title: As a developer, I want to rename files and folders, so that I can keep my project organized.

acs.text: Rename via moveEntry + tree refreshed; modal pre-filled with current name (TC-0053..TC-0058)

stories.title: As a developer, I want to delete files and folders, so that I can clean up my project.

acs.text: Confirmed delete calls deleteEntry + tree refreshed (TC-0059..TC-0065)
Cancel skips deleteEntry — no tree change (TC-0061)

stories.title: As a developer, I want to move files and folders, so that I can restructure my project intuitively.

acs.text: Move to valid destination calls moveEntry + onFileMove + tree reloaded (TC-0066..TC-0077)
Self-move and descendant-move blocked with error alert (TC-0073..TC-0074)

stories.title: As a developer, I want to open an integrated terminal, so that I can run commands without leaving the app.

acs.text: Terminal component renders the TERMINAL header and a prompt character ($) on mount
Terminal accepts a workingDirectory prop and displays it in the welcome output
Terminal input clears after a command is submitted
Virtual keyboard raises the terminal viewport on iOS via KeyboardAvoidingView

stories.title: As a developer, I want to run build and test commands, so that I can verify my code works.

acs.text: Submitting a command echoes it in the output prefixed with $
The onCommand callback is invoked with the exact command string on submit
Submitting an empty string does not add a line or call onCommand

stories.title: As a developer, I want to see colored terminal output, so that I can parse logs more easily.

acs.text: Command echo lines are rendered in a distinct color (green/success style)
Error/unknown-command lines are rendered in the error color (Coral #EF4444)
Regular output lines are rendered in the standard text color

stories.title: As a developer, I can use `touch`, `cp`, and `mv` commands to create and manage files.

acs.text: `touch <file>` creates a file if it doesn't exist; no-op if it does
`cp <src> <dest>` copies a file/directory
`mv <src> <dest>` moves/renames a file/directory

stories.title: As a developer, I can use `clear` to reset the terminal screen.

acs.text: `clear` resets terminal output

stories.title: As a developer, I can run `node <file>` to execute JavaScript files.

acs.text: `node <file>` executes JS via WebView engine, capturing console.log output
`node <file>` with `require()` returns a clear error

stories.title: As a developer, I can use `npm run <script>` to run package.json scripts.

acs.text: `npm run <script>` reads package.json and runs the matching script
`npm run <missing>` returns 'not found in package.json' error
`npm install` returns 'not supported' error

stories.title: As a developer, I can use `npx prettier` offline to format code.

acs.text: `npx prettier <file>` formats the file using pre-bundled prettier
`npx <unknown>` returns 'not available' with list of bundled tools

stories.title: As a developer, I see clear error messages for unsupported operations.

acs.text: All unsupported operations return specific, actionable error messages

stories.title: App.tsx uses TerminalWebView for the live terminal experience.

acs.text: App.tsx renders TerminalWebView instead of the deprecated Terminal stub

stories.title: As a power user, I want to open a command palette, so that I can run any action without navigating menus.

acs.text: CommandPalette renders a search input that auto-focuses on mount
CommandPalette renders all provided commands when the query is empty
Tapping outside the panel (backdrop) dismisses the keyboard

stories.title: As a power user, I want to search commands by name, so that I can find actions without memorizing locations.

acs.text: Typing a query filters commands to those matching label or description (case-insensitive)
When no commands match the query, a "No commands found" message is shown
Pressing Enter selects the first result in the filtered list

stories.title: As a power user, I want to see keyboard shortcuts in the palette, so that I can learn shortcuts over time.

acs.text: Commands with a shortcut field display the shortcut in a badge alongside the label
Commands without a shortcut field show no badge (no empty badge rendered)

stories.title: As a developer, I want to switch between light and dark themes, so that I can use the app comfortably in any environment.

acs.text: App renders using Deep Slate (#0F172A) background in dark mode by default
A theme toggle switches the editor and UI surfaces to Off-White (#F9FAFB) background
All text/background pairs in both themes meet WCAG 4.5:1 contrast for normal text

stories.title: As a developer, I want to change font size, so that I can read comfortably on my device.

acs.text: Pressing A+ in the editor toolbar increments the Monaco font size by 1pt
Pressing A- in the editor toolbar decrements the Monaco font size by 1pt
The current font size value is displayed in the toolbar between the A- and A+ buttons

stories.title: As a power user, I want to install extensions, so that I can enhance the IDE with tools I need.

acs.text: An extension manifest can be registered in ExtensionRegistry and retrieved by id
activateExtension registers the manifest and returns valid sandbox HTML containing the extension source
deactivateExtension removes the extension from ExtensionRegistry
Extensions run in an isolated WebView sandbox — the sandbox HTML wraps source in try/catch

stories.title: As a user, I want an About screen showing the splash and app info, so that I can identify the app version and credits.

acs.text: A ⓘ button in the status bar has a minimum 44pt touch target and is accessible via aria-label="About NomadCode"
Tapping ⓘ opens a full-screen modal displaying the splash image
The About modal footer shows the app version (from package.json) and copyright line
Tapping the ✕ close button dismisses the About modal with a fade animation

stories.title: As a user, I want the Monaco editor's syntax colours to match my selected NomadCode theme, so that my chosen look (Dracula, Nord, Solarized Light, etc.) is reflected in code colours — not just the app chrome.

acs.text: Each NomadCode theme ID (nomad-dark, one-dark-pro, dracula, monokai, nord, tokyo-night, nomad-light, github-light, solarized-light, catppuccin-latte, night-owl-light) is registered with monaco.editor.defineTheme on editor boot with a full IStandaloneThemeData palette (colors + token rules)
getMonacoTheme() returns the registered custom theme name (not vs / vs-dark) so custom colours are applied instead of the Monaco built-in fallback
buildMonacoHtml() injects a defineTheme call for every registered theme before the initial setTheme, so the editor never shows a vs-dark flash on custom themes
Switching themes in Settings or SetupWizard updates Monaco's active theme within one frame, with no unstyled interval
Unit tests confirm each theme's defineTheme payload contains non-empty colors.background, colors.foreground, and at least one token rule for keywords, strings, and comments

stories.title: As a project lead, I want a plan status dashboard, so that I can see project health at a glance.

acs.text: HTML page generated from RELEASE_PLAN.md
Filter by epic, status, priority, search
Epic→Story→AC→TC hierarchy view
All 7 Chart.js charts render on Charts tab
Cost table shows projected and AI costs per story
Traceability matrix links TCs to stories and ACs
GitHub Actions auto-deploys to GitHub Pages on qualifying commits
Claude Code stop hook appends cost rows to AI_COST_LOG.md

stories.title: As a developer, I want to sign in with my GitHub account, so that I can access private repositories and push code.

acs.text: Tapping "Sign in with GitHub" launches the system browser with the correct OAuth authorize URL
After successful OAuth callback, an access token is returned and stored in the keychain
The user's GitHub username and avatar are displayed in the settings screen after sign-in

stories.title: As a developer, I want my auth token stored securely, so that my credentials are never exposed.

acs.text: OAuth access token is stored in the platform keychain (iOS Keychain / Android Keystore), not in app storage or logs
Token survives app restart and is retrieved without re-authentication

stories.title: As a developer, I want to sign out, so that I can switch accounts or revoke access.

acs.text: Tapping "Sign out" deletes the token from the keychain and clears the user session
After sign-out, git operations requiring auth prompt re-authentication

stories.title: As a developer, I want to clone a GitHub repository, so that I can work on an existing project.

acs.text: User can enter a GitHub repository URL and initiate a clone to local storage
Clone progress is shown via a progress indicator; errors surface a readable message
Cloned repo appears in the file explorer immediately on completion
If the repository requires authentication and no GitHub token is stored, the user sees a clear message and a path to sign in (e.g. Settings)
When the network is unavailable or clone fails transiently, the user sees an actionable message; transient failures retry with exponential backoff up to three times (per AGENTS.md)

stories.title: As a developer, I want to stage and commit changes, so that I can record my work in git history.

acs.text: Modified files are listed in the git status panel with staged/unstaged indicators
Tapping a file checkbox toggles it between staged and unstaged
Entering a commit message and confirming creates a local commit
Git status panel interactive controls have accessibility labels and minimum 44pt touch targets (WCAG-minded)

stories.title: As a developer, I want to push and pull from remote, so that I can collaborate with my team.

acs.text: Tapping Push sends local commits to the remote using the stored OAuth token
Tapping Pull fetches and merges remote changes into the working directory
If push or pull requires authentication and no GitHub token is stored, the user sees a clear message and a path to sign in (e.g. Settings)
When offline or push/pull fails transiently, the user sees an actionable message; transient failures retry with exponential backoff up to three times (per AGENTS.md)

stories.title: As a developer, I want to view file diffs, so that I can review changes before committing.

acs.text: Tapping a modified file in the git status panel shows an inline diff view
Added lines are highlighted green; removed lines are highlighted red

stories.title: As a developer, I want to create and switch branches, so that I can work on features in isolation.

acs.text: Current branch name is visible in the status bar
User can create a new branch from the current HEAD via the branch panel
User can switch to an existing local branch without losing uncommitted changes

stories.title: As a free-tier user, I want clear limits displayed, so that I understand what the free plan includes.

acs.text: Free tier users can open up to 3 files simultaneously; attempting a 4th shows an upgrade prompt
Free tier label is displayed in the settings screen alongside an upgrade CTA

stories.title: As a developer, I want to subscribe to Pro, so that I can unlock unlimited files and advanced features.

acs.text: Pro subscription is purchasable via the native IAP sheet on iOS and Google Play Billing on Android
Successful purchase removes file-count limits and unlocks Pro-gated features immediately
Subscription status persists across app restarts via server-side receipt validation

stories.title: As a power user, I want to subscribe to Pro+AI, so that I can use AI-powered code suggestions.

acs.text: Pro+AI subscription unlocks AI Suggestions features in addition to all Pro features
Purchasing Pro+AI when already on Pro upgrades the subscription without double-charging

stories.title: As a Pro+AI user, I want inline code completions, so that I can write code faster.

acs.text: While typing, ghost-text completions appear after a 300 ms debounce
Pressing Tab accepts the current completion; pressing Escape dismisses it
Completions are only triggered for Pro+AI subscribers; other users see no ghost text

stories.title: As a Pro+AI user, I want an AI chat panel, so that I can ask questions about my code.

acs.text: A chat panel can be opened alongside the editor via a toolbar button
User can type a prompt referencing the current file and receive a streamed response
Chat history persists for the session and is cleared on app restart

stories.title: As a non-subscriber, I want AI features gated with a clear upgrade prompt, so that I understand what I'm missing.

acs.text: Non-Pro+AI users see a paywall prompt when attempting to open the AI chat panel
AI completion ghost text is hidden entirely for Free and Pro tier users

stories.title: As a release engineer, I want an EAS Build pipeline, so that I can produce store-ready binaries from CI.

acs.text: `eas build --platform all` produces a valid iOS .ipa and Android .aab without errors
Build configuration is defined in `eas.json` with a production profile

stories.title: As a release engineer, I want branded app icons, splash screens, and named APK artefacts, so that the app presents a polished identity in stores and on device home screens.

acs.text: App icon (1024×1024 NomadCode logo) is correctly referenced in `app.json`, `ios/Images.xcassets/AppIcon`, and all Android mipmap densities (mdpi–xxxhdpi WebP)
Splash screen displays the NomadCode logo centred on Deep Slate (#0F172A) background; `app.json` splash config uses `resizeMode: "contain"` and matching backgroundColor
Android APK build artefacts are named `NomadCode-debug.apk` and `NomadCode-release.apk` via `applicationVariants.all` in `android/app/build.gradle`

stories.title: As a release engineer, I want to submit to the iOS App Store, so that users can install on iPhone and iPad.

acs.text: `eas submit --platform ios` successfully uploads the build to App Store Connect
App metadata (name, description, screenshots, privacy policy URL) is complete in App Store Connect

stories.title: As a release engineer, I want to submit to Google Play, so that users can install on Android devices.

acs.text: `eas submit --platform android` successfully uploads the build to the Google Play Console
App metadata (title, description, screenshots) is complete in the Play Console internal track

stories.title: As a developer, I want to upload my project to cloud storage, so that I can access it from multiple devices.

acs.text: User can connect an S3-compatible bucket by entering endpoint URL and access credentials in settings
Modified local files are uploaded to the configured bucket automatically on save

stories.title: As a developer, I want to download remote changes on launch, so that my files are always up to date.

acs.text: On app launch with network access, remote changes are pulled and merged into local storage
Sync conflicts are surfaced to the user with a merge prompt before overwriting local files

stories.title: As a developer, I want syntax highlighting for Python, Rust, Go, and Swift, so that I can work in my preferred language.

acs.text: Python, Rust, Go, and Swift files receive correct syntax highlighting
Language is auto-detected from file extension; unknown extensions fall back to plain text

stories.title: As a developer, I want language-correct indentation and auto-close, so that the editor follows language conventions.

acs.text: Python files default to 4-space indentation; Go files default to tabs
Bracket and quote auto-close is active for all supported languages

stories.title: As a developer, I want to search across all project files, so that I can find any symbol or string quickly.

acs.text: A global search panel can be opened via the command palette or a dedicated toolbar button
Entering a query returns all matching lines across the project file tree
Results are grouped by file with file path, line number, and line preview shown

stories.title: As a developer, I want to navigate to a search result, so that I can jump directly to the relevant code.

acs.text: Tapping a search result opens the file and scrolls to the matching line
The matching text is highlighted in the editor after navigation

stories.title: As a developer, I want crashes reported automatically, so that I can fix production issues quickly.

acs.text: Unhandled exceptions and crashes are automatically reported to the configured crash reporting service
No PII, user file content, or credentials are included in crash payloads

stories.title: As a developer, I want performance metrics tracked, so that I can detect regressions before users do.

acs.text: Cold start time, editor input latency, and memory usage are tracked as named metrics
Performance degradation beyond baseline thresholds (§ 8 of PROJECT.md) triggers an alert

stories.title: As a developer using a foldable device (Samsung Galaxy Z Fold, Pixel Fold), I want the app to automatically expand to the full split-pane IDE layout when I unfold the device, so that I can use the entire inner display as a tablet workspace.

acs.text: When the device is unfolded and the inner display width is ≥ 768 dp, the app renders the split-pane layout (file explorer | editor | terminal) automatically
When the device is folded and the cover display width is < 768 dp, the app renders the single-pane layout automatically
The transition between folded and unfolded states occurs without an app restart — the layout reflows within the same session
Open files, unsaved editor content, and active terminal session are fully preserved across a fold/unfold transition
The app respects the device orientation in both folded and unfolded states — no forced landscape lock is applied (resolves BUG-0034)

stories.title: As a developer, I want the foldable layout to be tested against real device form factors, so that the split-pane expansion works correctly on all major foldable profiles.

acs.text: Layout renders correctly on Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 6 inner display (7.6", ~882 × 2176 px)
Layout renders correctly on Google Pixel Fold inner display (7.6", ~1840 × 2208 px)
Layout renders correctly on Samsung Galaxy Z Flip 6 cover display (3.4") and main display (6.7") — single-pane on both
`TabletResponsive` unit tests cover width breakpoints at 767 dp (single-pane), 768 dp (split-pane), and a mid-transition resize event

stories.title: As a developer, I want to create a new project from a starter template, so that I can start coding a common project type immediately without manual scaffolding.

acs.text: A "New Project from Template" option is available from the file explorer header and the command palette
At least 5 templates are offered: React Native (Expo), Node.js, Python, HTML/CSS/JS, and Blank
Selecting a template creates a new directory in the workspace with the appropriate starter files
The new project directory is immediately visible in the file tree after creation
Template scaffolding works fully offline — no network calls are made at project creation time

stories.title: As a developer, I want to connect to a remote Linux server over SSH from the terminal panel, so that I can administer servers and run commands remotely from my mobile device.

acs.text: The terminal panel provides a "New SSH Connection" option
The user can configure hostname, port, username, and authentication method (password or private key)
SSH credentials are stored in the platform keychain (iOS Keychain / Android Keystore) — never in plain app storage or logs
The SSH session streams stdout and stderr to the terminal panel in real time
Closing the terminal tab or tapping "Disconnect" cleanly terminates the SSH session on the server

stories.title: As a developer, I want the editor to apply language-appropriate indentation settings automatically when I open a file, so that code I type is formatted consistently with the conventions of that language.

acs.text: Python files use tabSize=4, insertSpaces=true, detectIndentation=false
Go files use tabSize=4, insertSpaces=false (hard tabs per gofmt)
Rust and Swift files use tabSize=4, insertSpaces=true
C, C++, C#, F#, Java, and Kotlin files use tabSize=4, insertSpaces=true
TypeScript and JavaScript files use tabSize=2, insertSpaces=true
Dart, HTML, CSS, JSON, YAML, and Markdown files use tabSize=2, insertSpaces=true
detectIndentation is always false — Monaco must not override configured rules
When switching tabs, indentation rules for the new tab's language are applied within the same message cycle as the content load

stories.title: As a developer, I want language-specific auto-close bracket behaviour, so that bracket and quote completion matches the language I am writing.

acs.text: Python files use autoClosingQuotes='languageDefined' (defers to Monaco's built-in Python grammar to avoid doubling quotes inside strings)
Ruby, R, HTML, and XML files use autoClosingQuotes='languageDefined'
All other supported languages use autoClosingBrackets='always' and autoClosingQuotes='always'
Switching from a Python tab to a Go tab updates autoClosingQuotes from 'languageDefined' to 'always'

stories.title: As a developer working with Zig, Dart, Lua, Elixir, C#, F#, R, Scala, Perl, PowerShell, Terraform, or Protocol Buffers files, I want NomadCode to recognise those extensions and apply a Monaco language mode and correct indentation.

acs.text: .cs → csharp, .fs/.fsx → fsharp, .zig → zig, .dart → dart, .lua → lua, .ex/.exs → elixir, .r/.R → r, .scala → scala, .pl/.pm → perl, .ps1/.psm1 → powershell, .tf/.hcl → hcl, .proto → proto, .ini/.cfg/.env → ini
Extension matching is case-insensitive (e.g. App.DART → dart)
Vue SFCs (.vue) fall back to html language mode (no dedicated Monaco grammar)
jsonc files map to Monaco language ID 'jsonc'; toml files map to 'toml'

stories.title: As an iOS user, I want to pick a workspace folder from iCloud Drive, On My iPad, or any File Provider (Dropbox, OneDrive, etc.), so that my NomadCode workspace lives where my other files are and syncs across devices.

acs.text: Custom native iOS module (Swift) wraps UIDocumentPickerViewController in directory-selection mode (.folder UTType on iOS 14+)
Selected folder URL is converted to a security-scoped URL bookmark (NSURL bookmarkData) and persisted so write access survives app relaunches
Before each git write, the module calls startAccessingSecurityScopedResource() and pairs it with stopAccessingSecurityScopedResource() after completion to avoid leaking entitlements
SetupWizard and Settings use the new picker via requestWorkspacePermission() — existing call site unchanged
When the picker is unavailable (e.g. user cancels, iOS < 14), the in-app SandboxDirectoryPicker is offered as fallback
Unit + E2E tests confirm clone/commit/push works in an iCloud Drive folder on a physical device

stories.title: As a developer, I want to create and switch branches from the mobile UI, so that I can manage feature work without needing a desktop.

acs.text: The Git panel exposes a "New Branch" action that accepts a name and creates the branch locally via isomorphic-git
A branch picker (bottom sheet on phone, sidebar section on tablet) lists local and remote branches and switches HEAD on tap
The active branch name is shown in the status bar and updates immediately after a switch without requiring an app restart

stories.title: As a developer, I want a merge conflict resolution UI, so that I can resolve conflicts without leaving the app.

acs.text: Files with merge conflicts are marked with a conflict badge in the File Explorer; tapping opens a 3-panel diff view (ours / base / theirs)
Each conflicted hunk has Accept Ours, Accept Theirs, and Accept Both buttons; accepting a choice writes the resolved content to the file
Once all conflicts are resolved, a "Mark Resolved & Stage" action is available; the conflict badge clears and the file is staged automatically

stories.title: As a developer, I want git gutter indicators in the editor, so that I can see which lines have been added, modified, or deleted since the last commit.

acs.text: Lines added since HEAD are marked with a green bar in the Monaco gutter; modified lines show amber; deleted positions show a red triangle
Gutter indicators update within 500 ms of saving the file (diff is computed against the HEAD version in isomorphic-git's object store)
Tapping a gutter indicator opens a compact inline diff popup showing the original line(s) with a Revert Hunk action

stories.title: As a developer, I want to stash and pop changes, so that I can context-switch without losing work in progress.

acs.text: The Git panel has a "Stash Changes" action that stashes all unstaged modifications with an optional message
A stash list shows all named stashes; tapping a stash offers Pop (apply + drop) and Apply (apply, keep stash) actions
Popping or applying a stash updates the working tree and refreshes gutter indicators without requiring a manual refresh

stories.title: As a developer, I want to see git blame annotations, so that I can understand the history of each line of code.

acs.text: A "Toggle Blame" action in the editor toolbar overlays each line with the commit hash, author, and relative timestamp
Tapping a blame annotation opens a detail sheet with the full commit message, author, date, and a "View Diff" link that opens the commit diff
Blame data is loaded asynchronously; a loading skeleton is shown while git log is computed so the editor remains responsive

stories.title: As a developer, I want to search and replace across all files in my project, so that I can do global refactors without a desktop IDE.

acs.text: The Global Search panel (EPIC-0014) gains a Replace field; entering a replacement term and pressing Replace All writes changes to every matching file simultaneously
A preview mode shows all replacements highlighted before confirmation; the developer can exclude individual matches with a checkbox
Replace supports regex with capture groups (e.g. $1); the preview updates in real time as the pattern changes

stories.title: As a developer using a hardware keyboard, I want standard shortcuts like ⌘S, ⌘`, ⌘N, and ⌘P, so that I can edit at desktop speed on a tablet.

acs.text: ⌘S saves the current file immediately; ⌘⇧S saves all open dirty files
⌘` opens/focuses the integrated terminal; pressing again cycles between terminal and editor
⌘N creates a new untitled file in the editor; ⌘P opens the Command Palette; all shortcuts are discoverable in a Keyboard Shortcuts help sheet

stories.title: As a developer, I want to fold and unfold code blocks, so that I can reduce visual noise when navigating large files.

acs.text: Monaco gutter displays fold/unfold chevrons for all foldable regions (functions, classes, blocks, comments)
A "Fold All" and "Unfold All" action is available in the editor context menu and Command Palette
Folded state persists per file within a session; reopening a tab restores the previous fold state

stories.title: As a developer, I want my code auto-formatted on save, so that I don't have to think about style consistency.

acs.text: Prettier (bundled as a WASM module) runs on file save for JS, TS, JSON, CSS, HTML, and Markdown files
Format on save is toggleable in Settings; when disabled, a "Format Document" action is still available in the Command Palette
Prettier config is read from the project root (.prettierrc, prettier.config.js) if present; defaults apply otherwise

stories.title: As a developer, I want breadcrumb navigation at the top of the editor, so that I can quickly understand and jump to any scope in a large file.

acs.text: A breadcrumb bar above the editor shows the file path segments and the current symbol hierarchy (e.g. File › ClassName › methodName)
Tapping any breadcrumb segment opens a picker listing sibling symbols at that depth; selecting one navigates the cursor to that symbol
Breadcrumbs update within one animation frame of moving the cursor to a new symbol

stories.title: As a developer, I want snippet expansion, so that I can scaffold common patterns with a short prefix.

acs.text: Built-in snippets for JS/TS (e.g. `clg` → console.log, `afn` → arrow function), Python (`def`, `cls`), and React (`rfc`, `useEffect`) ship with the app
Users can define custom snippets in Settings using a JSON schema; snippets support tab stops ($1, $2) and default values ($\{1:name\})
Snippets appear in Monaco's autocomplete list with a snippet icon; Tab accepts the first suggestion matching the typed prefix

stories.title: As a developer, I want Go to Definition, so that I can jump to where a symbol is declared without manual searching.

acs.text: Long-pressing a symbol opens a context menu with "Go to Definition"; tapping navigates the editor to the declaration line, opening the file if necessary
⌘Click (hardware keyboard) also triggers Go to Definition inline with VS Code muscle memory
If the definition is in an external node_modules package, a read-only preview of the declaration is shown in an overlay panel rather than opening the file

stories.title: As a developer, I want Find All References, so that I can see every place a symbol is used before renaming or deleting it.

acs.text: "Find All References" in the symbol context menu opens a References panel listing every usage grouped by file with line previews
Tapping any result navigates to that line in the editor; the panel stays open so the developer can step through each reference
Result count is shown in the panel header; empty results display a "No references found" state rather than crashing or silently closing

stories.title: As a developer, I want workspace symbol search, so that I can jump to any function or class by name without knowing which file it's in.

acs.text: The Command Palette gains a "Go to Symbol in Workspace" action (⌘T shortcut on hardware keyboard) that fuzzy-searches all exported symbols across all project files
Results show symbol name, type (function/class/variable), and file path; selecting one navigates directly to the declaration
Symbol index is built lazily on first use and refreshed on file save; an incremental spinner indicates a background refresh without blocking the UI

stories.title: As a developer, I want to select code and describe a change in plain language, so that I can make AI-driven edits without leaving the editor.

acs.text: Selecting a code range and invoking "AI Edit" (⌘K on hardware keyboard or long-press context menu) opens an inline prompt bar above the selection
After the developer types an instruction and submits, the AI returns a diff which is applied in a preview mode — Accept and Reject buttons confirm or discard
The inline AI edit is only available to Pro+AI subscribers; Free and Pro users see an upgrade prompt when invoking the action

stories.title: As a developer, I want to define project-scoped AI instructions, so that AI suggestions follow my team's conventions without me repeating them every session.

acs.text: A `.nomadcode/ai-rules.md` file in the project root is automatically loaded as system context for all AI requests in that project
The Settings screen exposes a global "AI Rules" editor for rules that apply across all projects; project-level rules override global rules where they conflict
AI rules are never sent to the AI provider if the session has privacy mode enabled (US-0091); a banner informs the user that rules are suppressed

stories.title: As a developer, I want AI-generated commit messages, so that I can write better commit history with less effort.

acs.text: The Git Commit panel has a "Generate Message" button that sends the staged diff to the AI and populates the message field with a conventional-commit formatted summary
The developer can regenerate or manually edit the suggestion before committing; the generated text is never committed automatically
The feature is gated behind Pro+AI; Free/Pro users see a disabled button with an upgrade tooltip

stories.title: As a power user, I want to choose which AI model powers my completions and chat, so that I am not locked into a single provider.

acs.text: Settings exposes a Model Picker with options for Claude (default), GPT-4o, and Gemini 1.5 Pro; selecting one updates all subsequent AI requests
Each provider requires the user to enter their own API key; keys are stored in the platform keychain (iOS Keychain / Android Keystore)
If the selected provider returns an authentication error, a non-blocking banner prompts the user to re-enter their key; the previous working provider is used as fallback

stories.title: As a developer, I want a TypeScript language server running in a WASM worker, so that I get accurate completions and type information without a server round-trip.

acs.text: TypeScript language server (typescript-language-server compiled to WASM) initialises in a Web Worker within 3 s of opening the first .ts or .tsx file
The LSP worker communicates with the Monaco editor via the Language Client protocol; completions, hover docs, and go-to-definition results are served from the worker
The WASM bundle is shipped with the app (not downloaded at runtime) to comply with App Store §2.5.2 and Google Play equivalent

stories.title: As a developer, I want IntelliSense completions, so that I get context-aware suggestions including imported symbols, methods, and types.

acs.text: Typing in a .ts file triggers LSP-powered completions within 200 ms; suggestions include local variables, imported symbols, and type members
Hovering (or long-pressing on touch) a symbol shows a hover card with the TypeScript type signature and JSDoc documentation if available
Completions rank exact-match prefixes highest; accepted completions auto-import the module if not already imported

stories.title: As a developer, I want real-time error and warning squiggles, so that I can see type errors without running the compiler.

acs.text: TypeScript diagnostics from the LSP worker are rendered as red (error) or amber (warning) squiggles under the relevant tokens in the editor
Tapping a squiggle opens an inline tooltip with the error message, error code, and a "Quick Fix" button when an LSP code action is available
A Problems panel (accessible from the status bar) lists all errors and warnings across open files; tapping an entry navigates to the offending line

stories.title: As a developer, I want my project indexed semantically, so that the AI understands the full codebase — not just the current file.

acs.text: On first open of a project, a background indexer computes vector embeddings for all source files (JS, TS, Python, Go, Swift, Kotlin) using a bundled embedding model
The index is stored on-device in SQLite (using the sqlite-vec extension); incremental re-indexing runs on file save, touching only changed files
A status bar indicator shows indexing progress; the AI features degrade gracefully (file-only context) until indexing completes

stories.title: As a developer, I want to reference @file and @codebase in AI chat, so that the AI can answer questions about specific files or the whole project.

acs.text: Typing @ in the AI chat panel opens a completion picker showing project files; selecting one appends its content (up to 8 k tokens) to the AI context
@codebase triggers a semantic search of the index and appends the top-5 most relevant chunks to the AI context automatically
Token budget for attached context is displayed in the chat input bar; exceeding the limit truncates the oldest attachment first and shows a warning

stories.title: As a developer working with sensitive code, I want a privacy mode that disables codebase indexing and AI context upload, so that proprietary code never leaves my device.

acs.text: A per-project Privacy Mode toggle in Settings disables all indexing and suppresses any code context from being sent to external AI providers
When Privacy Mode is on, AI completions and chat fall back to prompt-only mode; a persistent banner reminds the user that context is limited
Privacy Mode state is stored locally and is never synced to cloud storage; it defaults to off for new projects

stories.title: As a developer, I want to set breakpoints in the editor, so that I can pause execution and inspect state at any line.

acs.text: Tapping the Monaco gutter toggles a breakpoint indicator (red dot); the breakpoint is registered with the WASI debug adapter
When execution hits a breakpoint, the editor scrolls to the paused line and highlights it; a debug toolbar (Continue, Step Over, Step Into, Step Out) appears
Breakpoints persist across sessions in a per-project SQLite store; they are automatically removed if the file is deleted

stories.title: As a developer, I want a Watch panel, so that I can monitor specific expressions as I step through code.

acs.text: The Debug sidebar has a Watch panel where the developer can add any expression; expressions are re-evaluated at each breakpoint pause
Watch expressions that resolve to objects display an expandable tree; primitive values show inline
Adding a watch expression while not paused shows a "Waiting for breakpoint" placeholder; it evaluates automatically at the next pause

stories.title: As a developer, I want a call stack panel, so that I can understand how execution reached the current breakpoint.

acs.text: When paused, the Call Stack panel lists all active frames with function name, file, and line number
Tapping a frame navigates the editor to that line and updates the Watch and Variables panels to reflect that frame's scope
Async frames are visually distinguished from synchronous frames with an italic label and a different colour

stories.title: As a developer, I want a debug console, so that I can evaluate expressions and see console output while paused.

acs.text: The Debug Console panel streams console.log / print / fmt.Println output from the running process in real time
An input bar at the bottom accepts REPL-style expressions; results are printed inline in the console stream
Console output is colour-coded: stdout in default text, stderr in coral (#EF4444), and evaluated results in teal (#0D9488)

stories.title: As a developer using multiple devices, I want my settings synced, so that my theme, keybindings, and AI rules are consistent everywhere.

acs.text: Editor settings (theme, font size, keybindings, AI rules) are serialised to JSON and synced to the user's cloud storage bucket on change (EPIC-0012)
On app launch, the latest settings JSON is fetched and applied before the editor renders; a conflict (two devices edited at the same time) shows a merge diff
Settings sync is opt-in and off by default; a Settings screen toggle enables it and shows the last sync timestamp

stories.title: As a developer, I want to share a code snippet as a public link, so that I can quickly show colleagues a piece of code without setting up a gist manually.

acs.text: Selecting a code range and choosing "Share Snippet" generates a short URL pointing to a syntax-highlighted, read-only web page hosted by NomadCode's share service
The share URL is valid for 30 days and includes the language, filename, and highlighted range; the developer can extend or revoke it from Settings › Shared Snippets
Shared snippets contain no auth tokens or personal data beyond the code content; the share service stores only the snippet and expiry timestamp

stories.title: As a developer, I want to invite a collaborator to view my session in real time, so that we can pair-program without screen sharing overhead.

acs.text: The host generates a session invite link from the Collaboration menu; the collaborator opens it in NomadCode (or browser) and sees the editor state in real time (read-only by default)
The host can grant edit access to one collaborator at a time; cursors are shown with name labels in distinct colours; concurrent edits are resolved with operational transforms
The session is end-to-end encrypted (WebRTC DTLS); no code is stored on NomadCode servers — the relay only forwards encrypted packets between peers

tasks.title: Implement CodeMirror 6 in WebView for syntax-highlighted editing
tasks.notes: Superseded — Monaco Editor v0.45.0 adopted instead of CodeMirror 6 (better mobile WebView support). Syntax highlighting and language detection implemented in EPIC-0001/0013.

tasks.title: Wire FileSystemBridge to Expo FileSystem for real file read/write
tasks.notes: FileSystemBridge fully wired to expo-file-system (readFile, writeFile, listDirectory, etc). FileExplorer and App.tsx openFile() wired end-to-end. Path breadcrumb added to Editor. All ACs met.

FileExplorer renders files from FileSystemBridge listing

tapping a file calls onFileSelect with the file path
tapping a directory does not call onFileSelect
editor-path-breadcrumb testID is present when a tab is active

path bar is absent in the empty-state (no open files)
getLanguageForFile returns typescript for .tsx files
getLanguageForFile returns python for .py files
getLanguageForFile is case-insensitive for extensions
pressing A+ toolbar button increments font size display
pressing A- toolbar button decrements font size display
editor wraps content in KeyboardAvoidingView with correct behavior prop
KeyboardAvoidingView is present even in the empty state
pressing Save toolbar action does not throw when editor is ready
editor fires onSave message when Monaco sends a save event

Find toolbar button is reachable via getByLabelText

pressing a tab calls onTabChange with the tab path

Terminal renders the TERMINAL header label on mount
Terminal renders the $ prompt character on mount
Terminal welcome output includes the workingDirectory value
Terminal welcome output includes "NomadCode Terminal" banner
submitting a command clears the text input to empty string
Terminal wraps content in a KeyboardAvoidingView

submitting empty input does not add an output line
submitting empty input does not call onCommand
command echo line renders with commandLine style (green)
unknown command output uses error line style (Coral red)

pwd output renders as a plain output line
CommandPalette renders a search input placeholder
CommandPalette renders all commands with empty query
CommandPalette renders command descriptions when provided
pressing the backdrop does not crash the component
typing a query filters commands by label match

pressing Enter when no results does not call onSelect
commands with a shortcut field show a badge with the shortcut text
multiple shortcut badges are all rendered
tapping a command without shortcut calls onSelect without error
dark theme background token resolves to Deep Slate hex value

pressing A- decrements the displayed font size by 1

font size display updates after pressing A+
ExtensionRegistry.register stores a manifest retrievable by id
ExtensionRegistry.list returns all registered manifests

activateExtension returns HTML containing the extension source code

buildSandboxHtml embeds the vscode API shim
searchFiles yields nothing for empty query
searchFiles yields matching file results
searchFiles skips excluded directories

searchFiles skips unreadable files and continues
searchFiles applies glob filter

searchFiles caps at MAX_TOTAL_MATCHES
GlobalSearch renders search input and toggles
GlobalSearch shows grouped results by file
tapping result calls onNavigate with correct args
MonacoAssetManager scrollToLine calls revealLineInCenter
MonacoAssetManager scrollToLine applies deltaDecorations highlight
GitBridge categorizes statusMatrix and delegates clone/add/commit/push/pull/status/branches/diff to isomorphic-git
GitCloneModal validates URL, shows Settings link without token, clones with progress and bumps file tree revision
withNetworkRetry retries transient failures and maps auth/offline errors to readable messages
GitPanel loads status, toggles stage, commits, and lists branches with accessibility labels
GitPanel Push requires token alert; Pull and Push call GitBridge with OAuth token when present
GitDiffModal loads HEAD vs working diff and renders add/del line styling
GitPanel creates branch and checks out existing branch; useGitStore tracks branch and file tree revision

Backdrop press does not close the palette (CP-2)
AC-0046, AC-0048, AC-0053 had no explicit tests (CP-3)
Lines 79-83 of CommandPalette.tsx were uncovered by tests (CP-4)
EXPO_PUBLIC_GITHUB_CLIENT_SECRET bundled in app binary; env name mismatch (AUTH-1)
useAutoDiscovery('https://github.com') always returns null — OAuth button non-functional (AUTH-2)
Empty catch block in exchangeCode silently discards all OAuth errors (AUTH-3)
Landscape-locked app crashes on iPhone simulator when SetupWizard Modal is presented (SIM-1)
No swipe gesture trigger for command palette (CP-5)
node command exposes window.ReactNativeWebView to user scripts (TERM-1)
Silent undefined spread in FileBridge.handleMessage for unknown message types (TERM-2)
resolvePath does not normalise .. segments (TERM-3)
TypeScript strict-mode implicit any errors in terminal dispatcher (TERM-4)
npm run depth guard has no test coverage (TERM-5)
expo-crypto missing from package.json causes app entry not found crash on Android (ANDROID-1)
Terminal dist bundle stale — all commands return "command not found" (TERM-6)
No visible "New File" button in FileExplorer header (UX-1)
onFileCreate not wired in App.tsx — new files do not auto-open in editor (UX-2)
"File: New File" command missing from command palette (UX-3)
App.tsx wires Terminal.tsx stub instead of TerminalWebView (TERM-7)
git status throws "Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'bind')" in terminal (TERM-8)
Terminal cwd stays at "/" — ls / returns empty, touch creates files at wrong path (TERM-9)
sendToWebView injects JS object literal; receiveFromRN calls JSON.parse — all messages silently fail (TERM-10)
android/app/debug.keystore committed to version control (SEC-1)
getToken() has no reject path or timeout — git push/git clone hang forever (TERM-11)
git status reports newly-staged files as ?? (untracked) instead of A  (added) (TERM-12)
git init failure is misreported as "not a git repository" (TERM-13)
ENOENT error masking too broad — corrupted repos return wrong diagnostic (TERM-14)
Plan visualizer dashboard not updated since 2026-03-13 (DASH-1)
version-bump workflow fails with "Invalid format '0.1.1'" on GITHUB_OUTPUT (CI-1)
capture-cost.js Stop hook records zero tokens and cost for every session (TOOL-1)
[FIXED] editor.focus() not called after setContent — Android soft keyboard input silently dropped (EDITOR-1)
docs/LESSONS.md missing — plan visualizer Lessons tab always empty (TOOL-2)
applyLanguageRules message handler in MonacoAssetManager.ts is untested dead code (LANG-1)
App forces landscape orientation on all devices — breaks foldables and phones (COMPAT-1)
Soft keyboard types but characters do not appear in Monaco editor after creating a new file (EDITOR-1)
Markdown preview loads marked.js from CDN violating offline-first architecture (ED-1)
WebView security settings overly permissive (ED-2)
Hardcoded colors in Editor.tsx break light theme support (ED-3)
JSON preview uses hardcoded theme colors instead of dynamic values (ED-4)
Dead code — Terminal.tsx marked @deprecated but still exists (TERM-11)
GitBridge stub implementations throw errors at runtime (FS-1)
ESLint-disable comment in FileExplorer.tsx indicates code smell (FE-1)
StyleSheet created inside CommandPalette render (CP-6)
getMonacoTheme() returns only vs-dark/vs instead of named themes (THEME-1)
useTerminalBridge missing sendToWebView in dependency array (TERM-12)
Duplicate BUGS.md files — root and docs/ (DUP-1)
Emoji in toolbar icon causes potential rendering issues (ED-5)
Unsafe type assertion in useSettingsStore (SET-1)
App does not resize to fill screen on orientation change in Android emulator (LAYOUT-1)

recentActivity.summary: **Context:** PR #91 (Expo SDK 52→54 upgrade) merged yesterday. This session was a long bug-fix sprint covering the full list of issues found during iPad Pro simulator smoke-testing, plus significant git-integration polish driven by the EPIC-0008 test script.; ; 30 commits on `bugfix/sdk54-ui-fixes` off `develop`, grouped by area below.

**Context:** Multi-part session. Main feature work was EPIC-0008 (Git Integration) carried in from sessions on 2026-04-05, 2026-04-09, and 2026-04-12 — all uncommitted on `bugfix/session13-android-fixes`. Today's work was tsconfig fixes, dependabot consolidation, and full branch cleanup.
**Context:** Device testing session on Pixel Fold emulator (API 35) and iPad Pro 13-inch M5 simulator. Three Android-specific bugs found and fixed. Fold layout behaviour investigated and documented.
**Context:** Planning + full implementation of EPIC-0018 in a single session. Followed TDD strictly — failing tests written before every implementation step.
**Context:** Session recovered from a mid-session crash. Resumed from the plan to commit outstanding EPIC-0014 docs, merge PR #52, resolve open PRs, and fix all 13 open bugs in 3 grouped branches.

lessons.title: npm version stdout is multiline in newer npm versions
lessons.rule: Never capture `npm version` stdout directly for use as a value. Run it for the side-effect only, then read the clean version with `jq -r '.version' package.json`.
lessons.context: Attempting to capture `npm version patch` output with `$(...)` in a CI script to write to `$GITHUB_OUTPUT`.

lessons.title: Debug.keystore must be gitignored before expo prebuild
lessons.rule: Add `*.keystore` to `android/.gitignore` before running `expo prebuild`, or immediately after. Verify with `git status` before committing any `android/` scaffolding.
lessons.context: Running `expo prebuild` generates `android/` including `debug.keystore` (private key material); the generated `.gitignore` inside `android/` did not exclude it, so it was committed in PR #45.

lessons.title: injectJavaScript is silently dropped on hidden WebViews
lessons.rule: Never `injectJavaScript` before `onLoadEnd` fires. Always move initial bridge messages into the `onLoadEnd` handler.
lessons.context: `TerminalWebView` dispatched `SET_CWD` via `useEffect` before the WebView finished loading; the message was silently dropped and `cwd` stayed at `/`.

lessons.title: double-stringify is required when passing JSON through injectJavaScript
lessons.rule: When injecting a value that the WebView will `JSON.parse`, use `JSON.stringify(JSON.stringify(msg))` so the injected expression is a string literal, not an object literal.
lessons.context: `sendToWebView` injected `window.receiveFromRN(${JSON.stringify(msg)})`, passing a JS object literal. The WebView side called `JSON.parse(msgJson)` which coerced the object to `"[object Object]"` → SyntaxError → silent drop.
